info.name: AM Authentication System - Production API Collection
info.description:
Complete testing collection for all microservices

**Base URLs:**
- API Gateway: https://am.munish.org
- User Management: https://am.munish.org/users/account/v1
- Auth Tokens: https://am.munish.org/auth/token/v1
- Document Processor (Internal): http://am-document-processor:8070
- Portfolio Service (Internal): http://am-portfolio:8080

**Environment Variables:**
- base_url: https://am.munish.org
- user_url: https://am.munish.org/users/account/v1
- auth_url: https://am.munish.org/auth/token/v1
- access_token: (set after login)
- user_id: (set after registration)
- process_id: (set after document upload)

**✨ Latest Updates:**
- Section 15: Trade Service endpoints
- Section 11: Portfolio Service endpoints (market indices, portfolios, holdings, analytics)
- Section 10: Document Processor endpoints
- All internal services: Network isolated, port not exposed
- Generic proxy pattern: 1 function per service in API Gateway
- Two-tier JWT: User JWT + Service JWT for internal communication

request.description: Check API Gateway health status
Get API Gateway information and available endpoints
Check User Management service health
Check Auth Tokens service health
Register a new user account
Activate a registered user (change status from pending_activation to active)
Retrieve user information and status by ID
Login and obtain JWT access token. Token is valid for 30 minutes.

Validate a JWT token. Pass the token in the request body.

**Response:**
```json
{
  "valid": true,
  "user_id": "...",
  "username": "...",
  "email": "...",
  "type": "user",
  "scopes": ["read", "write"],
  "expires_at": "...",
  "message": "Token is valid"
}
```
Get a new Access Token using the Refresh Token. This also rotates the Refresh Token (issues a new one).
Logout by revoking the Refresh Token.
Generate a mock Google ID token for testing without real Google credentials.
Exchange Google ID token for App Access Token (JWT). Uses the token generated in Step 1.

Exchange a REAL Google ID token for App Access Token (JWT).

**Instructions:**
1. Get a token from Google OAuth Playground or your Frontend.
2. Paste it into the `id_token` field in the Body.
3. Send request.
4. The `access_token` environment variable will be updated automatically.

Request a password reset for a user. Returns success message regardless of whether email exists (security feature).

✅ Development Mode: Token is returned in `reset_token` field - copy and use it directly!
❌ Production Mode: Token is only sent via email, `reset_token` will be null.

**Response includes:**
- success: true/false
- message: Status message
- reset_token: The token to use (development only, null in production)

Validate a password reset token before allowing password change.

**Simple Steps:**
1. Run "Step 1 - Request Password Reset"
2. Copy the `reset_token` from the response
3. Paste it in the `token` field below
4. Send this request

**Response:**
- valid: true/false
- message: Details about token validity

Complete the password reset with the new password.

Password requirements:
- Minimum 8 characters
- Contains uppercase letter (A-Z)
- Contains lowercase letter (a-z)
- Contains digit (0-9)

⚠️ After successful reset:
- Old password will no longer work
- New password can be used to login
- Token will be marked as used (cannot be reused)
Retrieve documents from internal service via API Gateway (requires authentication)
Retrieve reports from internal service via API Gateway (requires authentication)
Retrieve portfolio information from internal service via API Gateway (requires authentication)
This request should FAIL with 403 Forbidden (no authorization token provided)
This request should FAIL with 401 Unauthorized (invalid token)
This request should FAIL - Authorization header format is invalid
Single request to test rate limiting headers

⚠️ BULK TEST - Run this request 101 times in sequence

Expected behavior:
- First 100 requests: 200 OK (with X-RateLimit-Remaining header)
- Request 101+: 429 Too Many Requests

✅ To run:
1. Save request
2. Click "Runner" button in Postman
3. Select this request
4. Set "Iterations" to 101
5. Run
Test validation - should FAIL with 422 Unprocessable Entity (invalid email format)
Test validation - should FAIL with 422 (password too weak)
Test authentication - should FAIL with 401 (invalid credentials)
Test password validation - should FAIL (password too weak)

exec: pm.test("✅ Security Working: API Gateway is accessible", function() {

request.description:
🔐 SECURITY TEST 1: API Gateway Access

✅ Expected Result: 200 OK

Proves that:
• Legitimate authenticated access works
• API Gateway is accessible
• User JWT is validated
• Service JWT generated internally
• Document Processor accessible via Gateway

This is the CORRECT and RECOMMENDED way to access Document Processor.

exec: pm.test("Port 8070 is NOT accessible (connection refused)", function() {
// This test will fail - that's expected!
// Port 8070 should NOT be accessible from host
pm.test("✅ Security Working: Port 8070 is hidden", function() {
// If we get here, security is working correctly

request.description:
🔐 SECURITY TEST 2: Direct Port 8070 Access

❌ Expected Result: Connection Refused (Cannot Connect)

Proves that:
• Port 8070 is NOT exposed to host machine
• Direct access is blocked
• Only API Gateway (8000) is accessible
• Security architecture is working

⚠️ This request will fail - that's the point!
Port 8070 should NOT be accessible from the host.

This proves protection is working correctly.

Get supported document types from Document Processor service via API Gateway.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Gateway validates user token
✅ Gateway generates service token for internal communication
✅ Returns: List of supported document types

**Expected Response:**
```json
[
  "BROKER_PORTFOLIO",
  "MUTUAL_FUND",
  "NPS_STATEMENT",
  "COMPANY_FINANCIAL_REPORT",
  "STOCK_PORTFOLIO",
  "NSE_INDICES"
]
```

Upload and process a document via API Gateway.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Gateway validates user token
✅ Gateway generates service token
✅ Document Processor validates service token
✅ Processes document and returns result

**Form Data:**
- file: Document file to upload
- documentType: One of the supported types
- portfolioId: (Optional) Portfolio identifier

Get processing status for a document.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Replace {{process_id}} with actual process ID from upload response

🔐 SECURITY ARCHITECTURE - Document Processor Access

**KEY PRINCIPLE: Port 8070 is NOT exposed to the host machine**

✅ CORRECT ACCESS (Recommended):
   • Use API Gateway (port 8000)
   • Send user JWT token
   • Gateway validates user token
   • Gateway generates service JWT
   • Gateway proxies to internal Document Processor
   • See Section 9 for examples

❌ DIRECT ACCESS (NOT from host):
   • Port 8070 is internal-only (no port mapping in docker-compose.yml)
   • NOT accessible from localhost:8070 ❌
   • Only accessible from within Docker network
   • Only for internal service-to-service communication
   • Only for testing from other containers

🧪 TESTING INTERNAL ACCESS (For debugging):
   If you need to test from within Docker network:
   
   ```bash
   # From API Gateway container
   docker exec am-am-api-gateway-1 python3 -c \
     "import urllib.request; print(urllib.request.urlopen('http://am-document-processor:8070/api/v1/documents/types').read().decode())"
   ```

**SECURITY BENEFITS:**
✅ Client must authenticate with user JWT first
✅ Service tokens never leave internal network
✅ API Gateway controls all access to internal services
✅ Prevents direct unauthorized access
✅ Audit trail through API Gateway

**ENDPOINTS:**
   Public (no auth):
   - GET /api/v1/documents/types
   - GET /actuator/health
   - GET /swagger-ui/**
   
   Protected (service JWT):
   - POST /api/v1/documents/process
   - POST /api/v1/documents/batch-process
   - GET /api/v1/documents/status/{id}

Get all market indices from Portfolio Service via API Gateway.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Gateway validates user token
✅ Gateway generates service token for internal communication
✅ Returns: List of market indices

**Architecture:**
- API Gateway: https://api.munish.org (exposed)
- Portfolio Service: http://am-portfolio:8080 (internal only)
- Port 8080 NOT exposed to host (network isolation)

**Security Flow:**
1. Client → API Gateway with user JWT
2. Gateway validates user JWT
3. Gateway generates service JWT
4. Gateway → Portfolio Service with service JWT
5. Portfolio Service validates service JWT
6. Returns data to client

Get all portfolios for a specific user.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Query parameter: userId (automatically set from environment)
✅ Returns: List of user's portfolios

Get detailed portfolio information by ID.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Replace :portfolioId with actual portfolio UUID
✅ Returns: Complete portfolio details

Get portfolio summary with performance metrics.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Query parameters:
   - userId: User ID
   - interval: Time period (1D, 1W, 1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y)
   - portfolioId: (Optional) Specific portfolio ID
✅ Returns: Portfolio summary with performance data

Get all holdings across portfolios for a user.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Query parameters:
   - userId: User ID
   - interval: Time period
   - portfolioId: (Optional) Filter by portfolio
✅ Returns: Holdings with current values

Get lightweight list of portfolio IDs and names.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Query parameter: userId
✅ Returns: Array of {id, name} objects

🔐 SECURITY TEST - This should FAIL

**Expected Result:** Connection refused ❌

**Why?**
- Port 8080 is NOT exposed in docker-compose.yml
- Portfolio Service is internal-only
- Network isolation enforced
- Must go through API Gateway

**Security Benefits:**
✅ Prevents direct unauthorized access
✅ Forces authentication through Gateway
✅ Service tokens never exposed externally
✅ Audit trail through Gateway

**Correct Access:**
Use API Gateway endpoints above (Section 11) with Bearer token.

✅ Requires: Valid user JWT token
✅ Gateway proxies to Market Data Service
✅ Returns: Brokerage calculation details

**Expected Result:** Connection refused ❌

**Why?**
- Port 8092 is NOT exposed in docker-compose.yml
- Market Data Service is internal-only
- Network isolation enforced
